summary: ANDROID 🟥 | FMD | Play/Pause button giving different outcomes
scenario: Find My Device tone fails to play after Play-Stop-Play sequence and rapid Play presses cause stacked noise with no Pause option on Poly Bluetooth device
description_clean: In the Find My Device (FMD) feature, pressing Play/Pause buttons on a connected device causes inconsistent behavior: (1) after pressing Play then Stop then Play again, no tone is heard; (2) rapidly pressing Play can cause tones to stack into very bad noise with no Pause button available, requiring the device to be turned off. Expected behavior is that the tone plays properly.
steps: 1. Open Poly Studio Mobile app on the phone.
2. Navigate to Home screen and ensure a Poly Bluetooth audio device is connected.
3. Go to Device Settings → [Device Name] → Find My Device (FMD).
4. Tap the Play button to start the FMD tone.
5. Tap the Stop button to stop the FMD tone.
6. Tap the Play button again to replay the FMD tone.
7. Observe whether the tone is heard on the second Play.
8. Tap the Play button rapidly multiple times in succession.
9. Observe the audio output from the device.
expected: The FMD tone plays clearly each time Play is pressed. Rapidly pressing Play should not cause overlapping or stacking tones; the tone should play normally without distortion or noise. A Pause/Stop button should be available to stop the tone at any time.
actual: (1) After pressing Play → Stop → Play again, no tone is heard on the second Play. (2) Rapidly pressing Play causes tones to stack into very bad noise, and no Pause button is available to stop the noise, requiring the device to be turned off.
